title: Today's Tech Brief: SoftBank's Big AI Bet in France
summary: SoftBank invests in AI data centres in France, Canada pitches itself to Trump, and more tech news.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech briefing. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you today. First up, SoftBank is making a significant investment in AI data centres in France. According to Reuters, the Japanese conglomerate plans to build up its AI infrastructure in the country. I'm curious to see how this will play out, as France is rapidly becoming a hub for AI development.

So, what's behind this move? It seems SoftBank is betting big on the future of artificial intelligence, and France is offering a perfect blend of talent and favourable business environment. This investment will likely drive innovation in the AI space, and could lead to some exciting new applications in the future. From self-driving cars to personalized medicine, the possibilities are endless.

Also making headlines is Canada's pitch to Donald Trump. Axios reports that the country is positioning itself as a strong partner for the US, with a focus on trade and economic cooperation. It's an interesting play, and one that could have implications for the tech industry. If Canada can strengthen its ties with the US, it could become an attractive destination for tech investment and talent. I wonder if this is a clever move to attract some of the tech companies that might be looking to relocate or expand.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
